mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and aggregate consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than fifty-five percent of months over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above three percent raises the cost of capital for corporations and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds three percent, S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty percent of triggered instances over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate and decline in industrial production indicate a joint deterioration in labor and supply conditions that compress corporate profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal occurs but S&P 500 forward twenty-day returns are positive in more than fifty percent of joint-trigger instances over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an acceleration in consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to increase interest rates more aggressively than equity markets have priced, reducing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, CPIAUCSL accelerations above zero point three percent do not precede negative five-day S&P 500 returns in at least fifty-five percent of instances over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative three-month change in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output and weaker corporate revenue growth, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO three-month change is negative but S&P 500 twenty-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty-five percent of triggered months over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based demand strength across consumer spending and residential investment, supporting higher corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month and HOUST increases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with negative sixty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of cases over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than twenty basis points over three months signals a rapidly tightening labor market and accelerating wage income, which boosts consumption expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines by more than twenty basis points over three months but S&P 500 five-day forward returns are negative in more than fifty-five percent of triggered periods over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative three-month change in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and falling corporate revenues, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS three-month change is negative but S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty percent of triggered months over any three-year rolling window.
